rationale: In the hypothesis development section on community building, the citing paper cites Banerjee et al. (2021) for a specific mechanism: Q&A options complement reviews, improve fit, and reduce uncertainty. The citation is not just a background list entry; it directly supports the logic behind H2a and the broader framing of reviewer-consumer interaction as a trust-building governance mechanism. Although the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's data or estimation approach, it relies on that prior finding to justify a core conceptual link.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Banerjee et al. (2021) as prior empirical support that Q&A features complement reviews and reduce uncertainty. This evidence helps ground the citing paper's argument that platform-facilitated community interaction can increase platform trust.
